extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won from a textbook DECEUNINCK Quick-Step leadout after THOMAS Geraint crashed at 30km to go, ending his GC hopes.
tactical_patterns: DECEUNINCK Quick-Step executed three-rider relay (ASGREEN-LAMPAERT-MØRKØV) proving decisive even when opposition teams knew it was coming.
BORA-HANSGROHE controlled peloton for SAGAN rather than letting breakaway gain time, revealing prioritization of stage over protecting yellow jersey energy.
FRANK Mathias attacked late to prevent bunch sprint despite local rider FRIESECKE in break, suggesting AG2R bet on Franck over teammate.
High-speed crash at 30km eliminated pre-race favorite THOMAS, indicating dangerous descent positioning before final climbs more critical than expected.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: First Tour de Suisse win shows he's peaking later in season than typical early-year dominance pattern
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Crash at high speed 30km out with hospitalization ends GC ambitions and reveals vulnerability in technical descents under fatigue
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Followed Viviani's wheel closely but settled for podium, suggesting team prioritized protecting yellow over stage hunting
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Second place moved him to second overall, showing climbing resilience unusual for pure sprinter in hilly stage race
rider_signals.FRIESECKE Gian: positive: Swiss national team rider lasted longest from break on local climbs, outperforming WorldTour riders in stamina
rider_signals.FRANK Mathias: neutral: Solo attack attempt to prevent sprint suggests AG2R lacked confidence in positioning for bunch finish
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: Leading three-man sprint train from distance demonstrates emerging domestique skill before becoming classics contender
rider_signals.ZEITS Andrey: negative: Crashed out at 30km alongside Thomas, removing Astana GC protection option
